prompt: The heritage-railway signal-box console crate stopped passing CI when we turned on the deny-warnings gate (`bash run_verify.sh` = `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo test`). rustc has apparently been trying to tell us about real problems for a while: typing `release` at the console comes back 'unknown keyword' even though there's a match arm for it right there in the source, lamp code 2 lights single yellow on the panel when the aspect table says double yellow, and the box journal has been printing `REFUSED description() is deprecated; use Display` instead of the actual refusal reason, which made for a fun conversation with the inspector. Get the gate green and those three behaviors right. One hard rule from our code-health policy: no `#[allow(...)]` / `#![allow(...)]` anywhere — fix each site properly instead of shushing the compiler. The tests pin the intended behavior; don't touch tests/interlock.rs or run_verify.sh, and don't restructure logic that already works.
notes: 5W2 item 2: rustc-only lint sweep (clippy-adjacent classes, no clippy needed), rustc 1.96.0, no attribute suppression (prompt forbids). Planted (all probed): unreachable_patterns x2 — command::classify has the 'release' arm AFTER the `_` catch-all (merge artifact; release parses as Unknown at baseline, behavior-restoring fix = reorder), aspect::from_code has `2 => Preliminary` swallowed by the `1..=2 => Caution` range above it (intended: 1 Caution, 2 Preliminary; tests pin code 2 = double yellow); irrefutable_let_patterns (let-else form) — plan::dwell_secs binds `entry.trim()` with a let-else whose else block is unreachable; fix = plain let (behavior identical: blank entries already fall to unwrap_or default, tests pin the fallback); deprecated x3 — str::trim_left + str::trim_right in plan::lever_label (fix: trim_start/trim_end or trim), Error::description in plan::journal_line — std's default description() returns the literal 'description() is deprecated; use Display', so the journal line is visibly garbage at baseline and the fix (Display) is behavior-restoring, pinned exactly. Baseline also fails plain cargo test (3 behavioral pins) in addition to dying at the -D warnings compile. Domain: railway signal-box lamp/lever console — distinct from all existing seeds (no rail-signalling seed exists; go-farebands is transit fares, different layer entirely).
